selected_important_turn.text: What about I take 2 of the packages and you take 1? I know teenagers also drink a lot of water haha 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is an early concrete allocation proposal that frames the negotiation as competitive over food. It shapes expectations (you take majority of food) and sets a zero‑sum tone that affects subsequent moves.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although it concedes one package, it stays firmly self‑focused and misses a clear integrative opportunity: the opponent had already signaled willingness to trade firewood for food. Proposing a simple 2/1 split without asking about or bundling other items (firewood, water) left value on the table and created a later need to renegotiate water fairness.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of a standalone 2/1 claim, explicitly elicit the other side's priorities and propose a linked trade or package deal (e.g., offer to take X if they take Y or to trade firewood/water for extra food). Show willingness to explore tradeoffs and quantify totals so both sides can see balanced outcomes.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — more integrative trades likely if you bundle items and elicit needs
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — clearer rationale for distribution and tradeoffs reduces imbalances
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — less back-and-forth when offers show concrete compensations across items
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — less competitive framing and more collaborative language builds trust

value2reason.Medium: I have hypothyroidism and get cold a lot.
value2reason.High: My friend has many allergies so the more diverse food the better.

value2reason.Low: We won't be having any late night festivities so I'm not to worried about extra firewood
value2reason.Medium: We plan on bringing out dog with us, it would be awesome to have a little extra in case he needs it
value2reason.High: I'm going to be camping with my teenage boy, you know how they be!

dialogue.text: Hey! For the camping trip it would be awesome if I could have 3 packages of food. My friend is gluten and dairy free and we need as many options as possible for her.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Aww I'm sorry to hear that, it sounds like it might be better to have her bring her own food. I'll be bringing my teenage son so it would be awesome to get any extra food for him that I can. You know how they be! 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: What about I take 2 of the packages and you take 1? I know teenagers also drink a lot of water haha 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'd be happy with 2 food packages and 1 water package. That way I can give my son some snacks and have extra water for our dog. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm happy to trade! If you take 2 food packages, I'll take 1 firewood and 1 water. That way, you get more food for your friend, I get warmth and hydration, and we both have balanced supplies. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with that deal! You get 2 food and 1 water, I take 1 firewood and 1 water. Let's confirm this split so we can both get ready for the trip. 🌲💧
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Actually, I think we can do better. Let me clarify: I’m happy to take 2 food packages and 1 water, but I’d like to trade the water for 1 firewood package. That way, you get 2 food and 1 firewood, and I get 1 water and 1 firewood. It balances our priorities and ensures we both have enough warmth and hydration. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think we're getting close! Let me just confirm: you take 2 food and 1 firewood, I take 1 water and 1 firewood. That way, you get more food for your friend, I get hydration and warmth, and we both have balanced supplies. Deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
